disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Thick white curd-like patches that leave a raw, easily bleeding surface when wiped
trigger_question_examples: Do the white patches wipe/scrape off leaving a raw red base?
Are they thick curd-like patches or firmly attached?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're thick white curdy patches; when the nurse wiped one it left a raw, slightly bleeding red patch.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete inability to swallow fluids or saliva for ~12 hours, with signs of dehydration
trigger_question_examples: When did the patient last drink, and can they swallow saliva at all?
How much urine have they passed today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She hasn't been able to swallow anything, even saliva, for about 12 hours and has passed very little urine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recent broad-spectrum antibiotics; frail, otherwise unexplained severe mucosal infection (immune status)
trigger_question_examples: Any recent antibiotics, steroids, chemotherapy or immune problems?
Why might she be prone to a severe mouth infection?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She just finished antibiotics for a urine infection; she's frail but no known immune condition.
